basalt-run-design-system-2-alert.name: Alert
basalt-run-design-system-2-alert.description: Alert communicates important information, warnings, or confirmations that require user attention.

basalt.usage: Use Alert when you need to interrupt the user's workflow with time-sensitive or critical information. Common contexts include form validation errors, system status changes, or action confirmations. Alerts should be brief and actionable—if a message requires substantial explanation or optional reading, consider a different pattern.
basalt.never: Don't stack multiple alerts in sequence — this creates cognitive overload and obscures priority. Use a toast notification system for sequential messages instead.
basalt.a11y: Alert must have role="alert" and aria-live="polite" to announce content to screen readers immediately upon DOM insertion, meeting WCAG 2.1 criterion 4.1.3.

basalt-run-design-system-2-badge.name: Badge
basalt-run-design-system-2-badge.description: Badge displays a compact, labeled status indicator for categorization or emphasis.

basalt.usage: Use Badge to label items with short, scannable metadata like status states, priority levels, or tags. In an issues list, Badges communicate priority and state at a glance without consuming significant space. Reserve Badges for read-only information; interactive labels belong in Button or Tag components.
basalt.never: Don't use Badge to convey critical information that requires user action — this creates false hierarchy and obscures call-to-action elements. Use Alert or ActionRequired component instead.
basalt.a11y: Ensure Badge has role="status" when conveying dynamic state changes that should announce to screen readers; use aria-label to provide context if the label alone is insufficient (WCAG 1.4.5 Images of Text).

basalt-run-design-system-2-button.name: Button
basalt-run-design-system-2-button.description: Button initiates an action or submission when activated.

basalt.usage: Use Button for primary user interactions such as form submissions, navigation, or confirming decisions. Prefer Button over links when the action changes application state or triggers a backend operation. In a payment flow, Button advances the user to the next step or completes a transaction.
basalt.never: Don't use Button for navigation to a different URL or page — this creates confusion between state-changing actions and routing. Use Link instead.
basalt.a11y: Button must have role="button" and support keyboard activation via Enter and Space keys per WCAG 2.1 Level AA.

basalt-run-design-system-2-card.name: Card
basalt-run-design-system-2-card.description: Card containers a discrete section of related content with optional visual separation and elevation.

basalt.usage: Use Card when grouping related information that benefits from visual containment, such as billing details in a settings panel or a single search result. Cards establish hierarchy and improve scannability in dense layouts. Reach for Card over semantic containers when the relationship between content groups matters more than the individual elements.
basalt.never: Don't nest Cards within Cards — it creates visual confusion and flattens information hierarchy. Use a single Card with internal sections or distinct parent-child containers instead.
basalt.a11y: Card must not introduce a landmark role; maintain semantic HTML by nesting heading, list, or button elements within rather than wrapping them. Screen readers should encounter card content directly without redundant container announcements.

basalt-run-design-system-2-checkbox.name: Checkbox
basalt-run-design-system-2-checkbox.description: Checkbox captures binary user input for a single option or as part of a group.

basalt.usage: Use checkboxes when users need to select one or more independent options from a set, such as filtering search results, accepting terms, or toggling feature flags. Reach for this component over radio buttons when multiple selections are valid. In settings panels, checkboxes clarify that each option stands alone.
basalt.never: Don't use checkboxes for mutually exclusive options — users expect each checkbox to operate independently. Use RadioGroup instead.
basalt.a11y: Implement role="checkbox" with aria-checked reflecting true, false, or mixed states. Support Space and Enter keys for toggle interaction, and ensure the clickable area includes both the input and its adjacent label.

basalt-run-design-system-2-input.name: Input
basalt-run-design-system-2-input.description: Input captures single-line text data from users through keyboard entry.

basalt.usage: Reach for Input when collecting focused information like email addresses, usernames, or search queries. Use it within forms where the expected response is brief and linear. Avoid Input for multi-line content or values requiring rich formatting.
basalt.never: Don't remove the visible label or rely solely on placeholder text — placeholder text disappears when typing and fails WCAG 3.3.2. Use a persistent Label component instead.
basalt.a11y: Pair Input with an explicit label using htmlFor/id binding. The input element maintains the implicit 'textbox' ARIA role; do not override it.

basalt-run-design-system-2-modal.name: Modal
basalt-run-design-system-2-modal.description: Modal presents focused content in a layer above the page, blocking interaction with background content until dismissed.

basalt.usage: Use modals for confirmations, form submissions, or information that demands immediate attention before proceeding. Open a modal when a user deletes an account or confirms a destructive action. Avoid modals for information users can safely ignore or return to later.
basalt.never: Don't nest modals within modals — this creates cognitive overload and breaks expected focus behavior. Use a Drawer or multi-step form within a single modal instead.
basalt.a11y: Modal must have role="dialog" with aria-labelledby pointing to the heading. Focus must trap within the modal on Tab, and Escape must close it. WCAG 2.1 Level AA requires keyboard navigation to be fully operable without a mouse.

basalt-run-design-system-2-select.name: Select
basalt-run-design-system-2-select.description: Select presents a list of options in a collapsed menu, expanding only when invoked.

basalt.usage: Reach for Select when users must choose a single option from a predefined list of five or more items. Use it for form fields like country, plan tier, or sort order where space is constrained. Avoid Select for fewer than five options; use RadioGroup or SegmentedControl instead.
basalt.never: Don't populate Select with dynamic options that change based on prior selections without re-rendering the component — users won't know the list has changed. Use a dependent Combobox instead.
basalt.a11y: Implement role="combobox" with aria-expanded toggling between true and false, and ensure arrow keys navigate options while Enter selects, per WCAG 2.1 1.3.1 Info and Relationships.

basalt-run-design-system-2-toggle.name: Toggle
basalt-run-design-system-2-toggle.description: Toggle switches a single boolean state between on and off with a single interaction.

basalt.usage: Use Toggle when users need to enable or disable a feature, setting, or mode. Prefer Toggle over Checkbox when the action takes effect immediately without requiring form submission—like enabling dark mode or activating a feature flag. Reserve Checkbox for form contexts where submission is required.
basalt.never: Don't use Toggle to select multiple items from a list — this creates ambiguity about the action scope. Use Checkbox group instead.
basalt.a11y: Implement role="switch" with aria-checked="true|false" to communicate state. Support Space and Enter keys for toggling without requiring mouse interaction.

basalt-run-design-system-2-tooltip.name: Tooltip
basalt-run-design-system-2-tooltip.description: Tooltip displays supplementary information on hover or focus, anchored to a trigger element.

basalt.usage: Use Tooltip to clarify abbreviated labels, explain icon meanings, or provide context for disabled form fields. In a data table with truncated values, a Tooltip on each cell reveals the full content without expanding the layout. Avoid Tooltips for information essential to task completion—that belongs in persistent help text.
basalt.never: Don't place critical information exclusively in a Tooltip — keyboard and screen reader users may miss it. Use a description field or help text component instead.
basalt.a11y: Tooltip must have role="tooltip" and maintain a aria-describedby relationship to its trigger element. The trigger must be keyboard-accessible and show the Tooltip on focus, not just hover (WCAG 2.1 Level AA).
